todos.title: Optimize scratch allocation and constant management in KernelBuilder
todos.context: Reduced scratch usage and potentially fewer instructions due to better constant reuse
todos.micro_goal: Implement the scheduled design contract within this scope: Improve scratch allocation strategy and constant caching to reduce redundant allocations
todos.expected_signal: Reduced scratch usage and potentially fewer instructions due to better constant reuse

risk_evidence.quote: Optimize scratch allocation and constant management in KernelBuilder
risk_evidence.explanation: This is a localized optimization to helper methods without changing control flow

todos.probe_plan: Enhance scratch_const to better cache constants and alloc_scratch to reuse addresses when possible.

probe_diff_contract.observation: Scratch usage should be more efficient without changing the generated instruction semantics

invariant_evidence: Scratch space is limited to 1536 words, so efficient allocation is important
Constant caching prevents redundant load instructions
preserved_invariants: Constant values must be correctly mapped to scratch addresses
todos.edit_scope: Improve scratch allocation strategy and constant caching to reduce redundant allocations

validator.failure_condition: CYCLES count increases or assertion failures in submission_tests.py

todos.correctness_rationale: This optimization only affects how scratch space is managed and constants are cached, not the actual computation. As long as addresses don't collide and constants are correctly mapped, correctness is preserved.
todos.fallback_plan: If optimization causes issues, revert to original allocation strategy.
todos.rollback_or_shrink_plan: Revert scratch_const and alloc_scratch to original implementations if any issues arise.

changes.reason: task-002: Optimize scratch allocation and constant management. The current scratch_const method is already quite efficient, but we can add a fast path that avoids redundant lookups or allocations if we track used constants more aggressively. However, looking at the code, the main inefficiency is actually in the loop where i_const is allocated every iteration. We can optimize by pre-allocating constants for the bat...

task-002: Optimize scratch allocation by pre-allocating constants for batch indices. This avoids repeated calls to scratch_const for the same index i across different rounds, reducing scratch space usage and potentially improving cache locality.

todos.title: Reduce loop overhead by unrolling or optimizing inner loop structure
todos.context: Reduced CYCLES due to fewer instruction bundles per batch item
todos.micro_goal: Implement the scheduled design contract within this scope: Optimize the inner loop structure in build_kernel to reduce instruction bundle count per iteration
todos.expected_signal: Reduced CYCLES due to fewer instruction bundles per batch item

risk_evidence.quote: Optimize the inner loop structure in build_kernel to reduce instruction bundle count per iteration
risk_evidence.explanation: This changes the control flow structure of kernel generation, potentially affecting correctness if not carefully implemented

todos.probe_plan: Implement a simple loop unrolling or instruction merging strategy that reduces the number of bundles generated per batch item.

probe_diff_contract.observation: The generated instruction sequence should produce the same results but with fewer bundles

invariant_evidence: The reference_kernel2 processes each batch item sequentially for each round
Index and value updates must match exactly
preserved_invariants: All batch items must be processed for all rounds
Index and value updates must match reference implementation
todos.edit_scope: Optimize the inner loop structure in build_kernel to reduce instruction bundle count per iteration

validator.failure_condition: CYCLES count increases or assertion failures in submission_tests.py

todos.correctness_rationale: By optimizing the loop structure while maintaining the same logical operations, we can reduce overhead without changing computational results.
todos.fallback_plan: If loop optimization causes issues, revert to original loop structure but keep VLIW packing from task-001.
todos.rollback_or_shrink_plan: Shrink to only optimizing constant loading and scratch allocation, reverting any loop structure changes if they cause issues.

changes.reason: task-003: Optimize KernelBuilder.build to pack instructions into VLIW slots. The original build() method created one instruction bundle per slot, leading to high cycle counts. By packing multiple slots of the same engine into a single bundle (respecting SLOT_LIMITS), we reduce the number of instruction bundles, which directly reduces cycles since each bundle takes at least one cycle.
